Friday, May 29 · 6:00 PM to 8:00 PM EDT

OneEleven

325 Front Street W, Suite 4 · Toronto, ON

dbt Meetup, Toronto Tech Week 2026 Edition!

Hosted by Daria S. and Moiz

Toronto dbt Meetup
4.7
19 reviews
Details

Welcome to the dbt Meetup, Toronto Tech Week 2026 Edition!
dbt Meetups are networking events open to all folks working with data. Talks predominantly focus on community members' experience with dbt, however, you'll catch presentations on broader topics such as analytics engineering, AI, data stacks, data ops, modeling, testing, and team structures.
To attend, please read the Health and Safety Policy and Terms of Participation: https://www.getdbt.com/legal/health-and-safety-policy

Toronto Tech Week is a citywide celebration of the people building what’s next. From May 25–29, 2026, founders, investors, and builders come together for hundreds of community-led events across Toronto, connecting tens of thousands of people around Canadian tech. Torontotechweek.com.

Agenda

6:00 > Arrive, Eat, Drink, and be Merry (aka Socialize)
6:30 > Speaker 1 and 2 presentations

Instructions:

Meet at entrance and someone will escort you for check-in

Speaker Sessions:
1st Speakers:

Josh Harris, founder of AnswerLayer.
Topic: Open Semantic Interchange shipped in January 2026 with dbt Labs as one of the co-leading vendors. OSI v1.0: what it is, why it exists, and what it means for dbt users.

2nd Speaker:

Daria Sukhareva, creator of kWwhat
Topic: Let’s chat: grounding ChatBI with dimensional models, semantic models, and rules - why your AI shouldn't be connecting dots.

Reach out if you're interested in volunteering/speaking!
➡️ Join the dbt Slack community: https://www.getdbt.com/community/🤝For the best Meetup experience, make sure to join the #local-toronto channel in dbt Slack (https://slack.getdbt.com/).
dbt is the standard in data transformation, used by over 40,000 organizations worldwide. Through the application of software engineering best practices like modularity, version control, testing, and documentation, dbt’s analytics engineering workflow helps teams work more efficiently to produce data the entire organization can trust. Learn more: https://www.getdbt.com/

Read more
Speaker
Josh Harris

Josh Harris, founder of AnswerLayer. Data science background, primarily in fintech. Built KOHO Financial's data platform from scratch in 2017/18 on GCP, before dbt was the default.

events.raw_text:

Wed, Sep 17, 2025 · 6:00 PM to 8:00 PM EDT

Village by the Grange Food Court (Dundas/University)

105 McCaul Street · Toronto, ON

How to find us
Analytics After Hours: Eat, Drink, Socialize

Hosted by Eddy Z.

Toronto dbt Meetup
4.7
19 reviews
Details

🍜 Join us for a casual evening of food, fun, and dbt!

We’re excited to bring together Toronto-based members of the dbt Community for a relaxed meetup. No slides, no talks—just good food and great conversations with other analytics engineers and data folks. Grab a table, order your favorite meal, and connect with both new and familiar faces in the local community.

This meetup is intentionally social and informal. Think of it as a chance to swap stories, share ideas, and strengthen connections in Toronto’s growing dbt network.

To attend, please read the Health and Safety Policy and Terms of Participation: [https://www.getdbt.com/legal/health-and-safety-policy ](https://www.getdbt.com/legal/health-and-safety-policy)

📝 Agenda
6:00pm - 6:30pm | Check-in /Order Food/Icebreakers (30 min)
6:30pm - 6:45pm | Welcome Remarks by Meetup Host (15 min)
👉 Call for speakers for next meetup
👉 Call for hosts/venue for next meetup
👉 Reach out if you are hiring (company & role) or interested in helping with the next meetup
6:45pm - 7:45pm | Dinner/Dessert/Networking (1 hour)
7:45pm - 8:00pm | Wrap up (15 min)

➡️ Join the dbt Slack community: https://www.getdbt.com/community/
🤝For the best Meetup experience, make sure to join the #local-toronto channel in dbt Slack (https://slack.getdbt.com/).
----------------------------------
dbt is the standard in data transformation, used by over 40,000 organizations worldwide. Through the application of software engineering best practices like modularity, version control, testing, and documentation, dbt’s analytics engineering workflow helps teams work more efficiently to produce data the entire organization can trust. Learn more: https://www.getdbt.com/

Happy Monday everyone!

Quick Reminder: Toronto dbt Meetup is this Wednesday ! Don’t forget to RSVP if you haven’t already.

Here’s what to expect:

Some catered food & drinks will be provided (Halal/Vegetarian Options)
Grab a bingo card and connect with other data folks in the city.
A casual, social evening — no slides, no talks. Just great conversations with the Toronto analytics community.

Time: Wednesday, Sept 17 | 6:00–8:00 PM
Venue: OCAD Food Court

See you all soon!
